workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and authority framing
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, desired artifact, migration phase, planning horizon, workload criticality, target provider, migration owner, rollback owner, approval authority, source precedence, and adjacent-lane indicators before producing guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Inventory, dependency, and system-of-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect workload inventory, dependency maps, source and target architecture records, migration tool data, cloud inventories, DNS, network, identity, backup, monitoring, change, capacity, cost, policy, and owner records from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare migration demand with migration team capacity, maintenance windows, blackout dates, dependency order, replication status, RTO/RPO, backup coverage, restore evidence, quotas, network capacity, tenant isolation, cost, licensing, and rollback constraints.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source selection and precedence review
workflow_stages.description: Choose current provider, Kubernetes, Terraform, contingency, control, FinOps, and tenant sources; record freshness, source scope, conflicts, decay policy, and precedence gaps.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Migration strategy and wave recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Recommend migration patterns, wave order, schedule, allocation, validation gates, deferrals, rollback checkpoints, owners, downstream impact, and assumption boundaries under approved priority rules.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cutover, rollback, validation, and handoff artifact production
workflow_stages.description: Produce cutover runbooks, readiness evidence summaries, test outcome summaries, migration troubleshooting playbooks, operational handoff checklists, deployment notes, commercial notes, constraint logs, and approval-required escalation packets tied to evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, refusal, and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when tenant facts, source freshness, source precedence, migration evidence, approval authority, incident ownership, safety, policy, legal, procurement, security, compliance, or adjacent-specialist ownership prevents safe advisory output.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: migration intake record with request intent, desired artifact, migration phase, planning horizon, owner, and authority map
workload inventory, dependency map, owner map, service criticality, business impact, traffic profile, source topology, and target-state constraints
migration assessment records, discovery outputs, dependency analysis, migration backlog, wave plan, migration factory capacity, issue log, and exception register
cloud account, subscription, project, cluster, region, zone, resource, quota, capacity, tenancy, landing-zone, network, DNS, firewall, identity, and access inventory
replication status, migration tool logs, test launch evidence, validation tests, health-check results, synthetic tests, performance baselines, and cutover evidence
backup policies, vault or repository configuration, retention settings, restore points, restore-test evidence, DR exercise records, and rollback plans
monitoring dashboards, alert policies, metric and log extracts, tracing samples, incident records, post-cutover issue records, and operational handoff notes
Terraform state and backend records, IaC repositories, plan and apply logs, drift reports, Kubernetes cluster component records, and manifest inventories where relevant
maintenance windows, change tickets, release calendars, blackout dates, cutover approvals, rollback approvals, risk records, and communication plans
cost and usage reports, capacity forecasts, licensing constraints, FinOps optimization requests, spend-owner handoff notes, and commercial packaging evidence

migration strategy memos, cutover runbooks, readiness evidence summaries, deployment notes, commercial packaging notes, schedule recommendations, constraint logs, and approval-required escalation notes
systems_of_record: migration inventory and assessment systems: AWS Migration Hub, AWS Application Migration Service, Azure Migrate, Google Cloud Migration Center, CMDB, discovery tooling, dependency maps, or tenant equivalent
cloud provider inventory and configuration records: AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, Google Cloud Asset Inventory, Kubernetes API, Terraform state, Git repositories, or tenant equivalent
network, identity, and access records: DNS, IPAM, firewall rules, route tables, VPN or interconnect records, identity tenant records, role assignments, service accounts, and privileged access logs

observability and validation systems: Amazon CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Google Cloud Monitoring and Logging, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry collectors, log archives, health-check dashboards, synthetic tests, and test evidence stores
backup and recovery systems: AWS Backup, Azure Backup, Google Backup and DR, snapshots, replication tools, backup vaults, restore-test records, DR exercise logs, and contingency plan repository

incident and change systems: ITSM, paging platform, post-cutover issue tracker, post-incident review repository, change calendar, release calendar, cutover calendar, approval matrix, exception register, and owner directory

planning and capacity systems: migration backlog, work intake queue, wave board, maintenance-window calendar, service inventory, migration factory capacity records, utilization and quota records, cost and usage reports, and FinOps records

policy and evidence stores: tenant standards, architecture decision records, runbooks, contractual constraints, regulatory mappings, data classification records, risk register, audit evidence repository, and commercial packaging repository

allowed: classify requests as in scope, blocked, out of scope, or adjacent-lane for cloud migration

draft and review migration readiness assessments, target-state architecture guidance, wave plans, cutover runbooks, rollback checkpoints, validation gates, troubleshooting plans, operational handoff notes, deployment notes, and commercial packaging notes
recommend schedule, priority, or allocation options under approved migration constraints and explicitly state downstream impact
produce constraint logs, evidence checklists, readiness notes, source-conflict summaries, and approval-required escalation packets
prepare migration troubleshooting and evidence collection guidance while preserving incident command, network execution, identity administration, and customer-notification boundaries
escalate: required workload inventory, dependency map, migration tool data, replication evidence, backup records, restore-test evidence, telemetry, change records, owner map, approval matrix, source freshness, or tenant policy records are missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
migration demand exceeds capacity and an authorized owner must approve deferral, staffing, budget, scope, or risk tradeoffs
priorities conflict and no approved decision rule or owner is available
public source, provider guidance, tenant policy, contract, regulatory obligation, change authority, or human owner instruction materially conflict
final cutover, rollback execution, production change, incident command, customer notification, risk acceptance, legal ruling, compliance signoff, or procurement approval is requested
provider-specific behavior is requested without current official source support
refuse: application feature code, UI engineering, business logic, database schema redesign, app architecture, SDLC, or application-only release engineering requests

endpoint administration, identity product administration, IAM policy ownership, network operations execution, database administration, storage administration, or provider implementation work outside migration-enablement guidance

cybersecurity incident command, forensic investigation, vulnerability management, penetration testing, security-control attestation, legal advice, compliance declarations, final budget or staffing approval, procurement decision, risk acceptance, or production execution
generic cloud migration best-practice answers that ignore workload inventory, dependency systems, migration records, provider footprint, systems of record, source freshness, and authority constraints

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic migration advice replaces named cloud migration evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Recommendations can ignore workload dependencies, migration tool records, provider footprint, source systems, blackout windows, tenant isolation, source freshness, and approval boundaries.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require migration inventory, dependency, assessment, replication, backup, monitoring, cutover, rollback, capacity, policy, and owner records before deterministic guidance.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Migration wave recommendation is treated as final operational approval
domain_failure_modes.impact: The agent could imply authority over final cutover, staffing, budget, rollback execution, risk acceptance, or customer-impact decisions.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Separate recommendation from approval and route final authority to change, risk, platform, migration, incident, or human owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Incomplete dependency map causes unsafe sequencing
domain_failure_modes.impact: Hidden DNS, network, cache, message bus, identity, database, or shared-service dependencies can break workloads during migration.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Treat dependency gaps as blockers, require system-of-record evidence, and use confidence-tagged wave planning.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Restore and rollback readiness is inferred from replication success alone
domain_failure_modes.impact: Cutover can proceed without tested recovery, backup validation, rollback authority, or DR evidence.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require restore-test history, RTO/RPO authority, rollback checkpoints, backup evidence, and contingency evidence before readiness claims.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Cost optimization overrides reliability, isolation, or recovery constraints
domain_failure_modes.impact: Savings requests can weaken SLOs, RTO/RPO, tenant isolation, recovery, or validation without authorized tradeoff approval.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Frame cost as a tradeoff, route spend and risk approval to owners, and require tenant-approved priority rules.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Adjacent implementation work is absorbed by the cloud migration lane
domain_failure_modes.impact: Specialist overreach can produce poor application, landing-zone, Kubernetes, Terraform, network, identity, endpoint, or security decisions outside authority.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Reject or route adjacent work while preserving migration evidence for the next owner.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Public source and tenant policy conflict is hidden
domain_failure_modes.impact: The recommendation can violate contract, regulated workload policy, data residency, privacy, or tenant control requirements.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record conflict, preserve public-source traceability, and escalate precedence to authorized owner.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Migration incident evidence turns into security incident ownership
domain_failure_modes.impact: The lane could overreach into credential compromise, forensics, security response, or customer notification without authority.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve migration telemetry and rollback evidence, then route incident command and investigation to security owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Industry-specific prerequisite or evidence gap
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
core implementation or architecture guidance
operations and troubleshooting guidance
resilience and failure scenarios
cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
Demand exceeds available capacity
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule
Request for final approval outside lane authority
Scenario proving industry-specific in-scope execution
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not treat the industry name as cosmetic; it must materially change the pack.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

research_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
research_questions.answer: Cloud migration guidance is governed by workload inventory, dependency maps, migration assessment tools, provider migration tooling, cloud inventories, landing-zone records, DNS and network records, identity dependency records, Terraform state, Kubernetes records, backup and restore-test evidence, monitoring and health-check evidence, change calendars, migration wave boards, cutover runbooks, approval matrices, tenant policy, data classification, contracts, risk registers, cost and capacity records, and audit evidence stores.

research_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
research_questions.answer: Cloud migration has provider-specific migration tooling, dependency graph risk, target landing-zone constraints, replication lag, cutover and rollback timing, RTO/RPO and restore-test evidence, quota and capacity limits, DNS and network transitions, identity dependencies, cloud actor responsibility boundaries, tenant isolation, data residency, and source-precedence conflicts that generic planning advice cannot safely infer.

research_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
research_questions.answer: The lane cannot approve final cutover, rollback execution, staffing, budget, procurement, legal conclusions, compliance attestation, privacy or residency rulings, incident command, risk acceptance, application design or code, landing-zone guardrail ownership, Kubernetes implementation, Terraform module coding, network operations execution, identity administration, endpoint administration, database administration, or security investigation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific provider footprint, dependency maps, policies, contracts, and systems of record
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack requires tenant retrieval or tenant-supplied facts at runtime and escalates when missing facts would change the migration recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider service availability, quotas, pricing, migration-tool behavior, and preview feature behavior
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack records refresh intervals and requires current official source validation before provider-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated workload overlays and jurisdiction-specific migration rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The cross-industry pack records privacy, contractual, data residency, and regulatory caveats but routes legal, compliance, privacy, or attestation decisions to authorized owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final cutover, rollback, risk, staffing, budget, and customer notification authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The specialist provides evidence-backed recommendations and escalation notes but refuses final approval or execution authority.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers named cloud migration guidance, provider migration tooling, workflow references, evidence and control standards, terminology, cost allocation, and failure handling. Remaining ambiguities are tenant-specific runtime inputs or adjacent authority decisions rather than blockers to a validated spec pack.
